numerical_constraints.name: Groene oever Profiel A-A' total section width
numerical_constraints.condition: Cross-section A-A' of the Groene oever (auto te gast eenrichting). Sum of visible sub-dimensions: 3.76 + 4.73 + 6.15 + 3.85 + 13.70 + 12.30 + 5.00 + 4.70 + 0.75 = ~55.44 m (or per aggregate labels: 36.45 + 14.95 + 0.75 + 3.76 = partial spans shown).

reasons: Dimensions are clearly labelled in the cross-section drawing but this is a beeldkwaliteitplan (image quality plan), which is informative not binding.
The aggregate spans 36.45 m and 14.95 m are legible in the drawing.
No regels clause backs these values; they reflect design intent for public realm profile.

numerical_constraints.notes: The drawing shows a cross-section with the following labelled sub-widths from left to right: 3.76, 4.73, 6.15, 3.85, 13.70, 12.30, 5.00, 4.70, 0.75 m. Aggregate dimensions shown: 36.45 m and 14.95 m. These appear to be public-realm/street profile dimensions, not binding numerical constraints from regels. Extracted as informative reference from beeldkwaliteitplan.

narrative_constraints.statement: The Groene oever cross-section A-A' is designed as a 'auto te gast eenrichting' (car as guest, one-way traffic) street profile, prioritising pedestrian and green space over vehicle movement.

provenance.quoted_text: Groene oever - Profiel A-A' (auto te gast eenrichting)

reasons: Clearly stated in the section title. 'Auto te gast' is a recognised Dutch urban design typology for shared space with pedestrian priority.
Beeldkwaliteitplan is informative/guiding, not legally binding like regels.

urban_intent_passages: Groene oever - Profiel A-A' (auto te gast eenrichting): cross-section showing a green waterfront street profile with one-way car access, broad green zones, water feature, and pedestrian/cycling space totalling approximately 36.45 m + 14.95 m in width.
